commands.review_summary:
The branch removes package/catalog cursor query-key metadata, cursorReset propagation, and stale-query reset tests while retaining the family-scoped downloads pagination fix and focused coverage for it.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a PR simplifying current cursor behavior, not a standalone bug report. The relevant behavior is source-verifiable in the package listing and HTTP catalog cursor paths.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human review should decide whether removing cursor query-key resets is acceptable for API pagination compatibility; there is no narrow automation repair to queue.

target.title: fix: simplify package listing cursors
target.body:
## Summary

- Removes the package/catalog cursor query-key hardening added in #2568.
- Removes `cursorReset` propagation from package list queries into the HTTP catalog merger.
- Keeps the real fix from #2568: download-sorted plugin family pages still use the family-scoped downloads index.
- Keeps tests for the retained behavior, including the family index binding and the non-indexed filter fallback scan.

## Why

The stale-cursor case is low impact and self-recovers once the client starts a fresh listing request. Keeping query-key metadata in every cursor adds more code and test weight than the case is worth.

## Validation

- `bun run test -- convex/packages.public.test.ts convex/httpApiV1.handlers.test.ts` - passed, 505 tests.
- `bun run format:check -- convex/packages.ts convex/httpApiV1/packagesV1.ts convex/packages.public.test.ts convex/httpApiV1.handlers.test.ts` - passed.
- `bunx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` - passed.
- `git diff --check` - passed.

## Not clean locally

- `bun run ci:unit` was attempted. It failed in this worktree before a useful full-suite signal because Vitest workers hit `ERR_REQUIRE_ESM` from `html-encoding-sniffer` requiring `@exodus/bytes/encoding-lite.js`; the incomplete run then failed coverage thresholds.
target.author: jesse-merhi
